name: Leonardo da Vinci
one_liner: He fused art and curiosity into a lifelong habit of invention.

name: Michelangelo
one_liner: He turned discipline into masterpieces that still move people.

name: Frida Kahlo
one_liner: She transformed pain into art with fearless honesty.

name: Vincent van Gogh
one_liner: He kept creating through hardship, leaving a new way to see.

name: Johann Sebastian Bach
one_liner: He showed how structure can become pure emotion.

name: Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart
one_liner: He proved play and precision can coexist in brilliance.

name: Ludwig van Beethoven
one_liner: He kept composing against the odds and expanded what music could be.

name: William Shakespeare
one_liner: He captured human nature so well that his ideas still feel modern.

name: Jane Austen
one_liner: She used wit and clarity to tell timeless truths about people.

name: Miguel de Cervantes
one_liner: He turned setbacks into a story that reshaped literature.

name: Victor Hugo
one_liner: He wrote with moral force and pushed culture toward empathy.

name: Fyodor Dostoevsky
one_liner: He faced extremes and wrote with a depth that changed fiction.

name: Leo Tolstoy
one_liner: He searched for meaning and wrote stories that still challenge us.

name: Rabindranath Tagore
one_liner: He blended poetry and philosophy to inspire human dignity.

name: Murasaki Shikibu
one_liner: She wrote one of the earliest great novels, building a new form of storytelling.

name: Toni Morrison
one_liner: She reshaped modern literature with language that refuses to look away.

name: James Baldwin
one_liner: He used words as a mirror and a compass for justice.

name: Octavia E. Butler
one_liner: She imagined futures that still teach courage and adaptability.

name: Socrates
one_liner: He proved that asking better questions can change a life.

name: Aristotle
one_liner: He tried to map knowledge itself, inspiring centuries of thinking.

name: Confucius
one_liner: He taught that character and duty can outlast any era.

name: Mary Wollstonecraft
one_liner: She argued for equal dignity with clarity that still resonates.

name: Simone de Beauvoir
one_liner: She challenged assumptions and expanded how people think about freedom.

name: Hannah Arendt
one_liner: She insisted on thinking clearly when the world becomes loud.

name: Ibn Rushd (Averroes)
one_liner: He defended reason and helped carry ideas across cultures.
